task: DLD-data-driven Buildings/Areas/Projects layer: backend models + endpoints + daily refresh, then wire frontend Compare/search/Pulse to use them.

stages.spec:
Stage 1 — Backend (binayah-api): DLD data layer + endpoints.

WORKING DIR: /Users/zoop/binayah-api
SOURCE FILES (on user's local Mac):
- /Users/zoop/binayah-properties/buildings.json (5,781 building aggregates: city, area, masterProject, building, sales, rents, units)
- /Users/zoop/binayah-properties/Units.csv (2,376,923 rows, 831MB; per-unit DLD data)

EXISTING:
- DldTransaction model + collection (55K records, gateway.dubailand.gov.ae imports, daily cron 03:00 UTC)
- /api/market-data/transactions (analytics aggregation)

TASKS:

1) Add new Mongoose models:
   - DldBuilding: { slug (unique), name, area, masterProject, city, sales, rents, units, avgPpsf, avgPrice, propertyTypes[], roomTypeBreakdown{studio,1br,2br,3br,4br,5plus}, freeholdShare, lastAggregatedAt }
     slug = lowercase + dashes from `name` field (kebab-case). Buildings with duplicate names get suffixed by area: "burj-vista--burj-khalifa".
   - DldArea: { slug (unique), name, city, totalUnits, totalSales, totalRents, buildingCount, masterProjects[], avgPpsf, avgPrice, lastAggregatedAt }
   - DldMasterProject: { slug (unique), name, city, areas[], buildingCount, totalUnits, totalSales }
   Indexes on: slug (unique), area, sales (descending), units (descending), masterProject.

2) Create scripts/dld-import.ts (run-once seed + idempotent re-runs):
   - Imports buildings.json — upsert ~5,800 DldBuilding records (slug as match key)
   - Streams Units.csv (use csv-parse/stream — DO NOT load 871MB into memory) — for each row, extract building name + master project + area + room type + actual_area + is_free_hold; accumulate per-building aggregates in a JS Map; bulk-write building aggregates after stream ends
   - Run after Units.csv stream: aggregate Areas + MasterProjects from DldBuilding collection
   - Total runtime expected: 5-15 min for 2.37M rows
   - Add npm script: "dld-import": "tsx scripts/dld-import.ts"
   - Add a comment explaining how to trigger on Render: SSH to instance, copy CSV via scp or download to /tmp, run npm run dld-import

3) Create scripts/dld-refresh.ts (daily, fast, no CSV needed):
   - Runs aggregations against DldTransaction collection (already updated daily)
   - For each building name in DldTransaction.distinct("building"), compute: sales count (last 12mo), avgPpsf, avgPrice from $group pipeline
   - Update matching DldBuilding records (set sales/avgPpsf/avgPrice/lastAggregatedAt)
   - Same for DldArea aggregations from DldTransaction
   - Add npm script: "dld-refresh": "tsx scripts/dld-refresh.ts"
   - Wire into existing dld-worker.ts cron — after each daily fetchDldTransactions run, ALSO call this refresh.

4) Add new routes file src/routes/dld.ts mounted at /api/dld:
   GET /api/dld/buildings — query params: q (search), area, masterProject, sortBy (sales|units|rents|avgPpsf), order (asc|desc), limit (max 100), offset
     Returns: { results: [...], total, hasMore }. Each result has all DldBuilding fields.
   GET /api/dld/buildings/:slug — single building detail; also include last 10 transactions from DldTransaction matching this building name (computed live).
   GET /api/dld/areas — same shape; sortBy options: totalSales, totalUnits, buildingCount.
   GET /api/dld/areas/:slug — single area + top 20 buildings in this area + recent transactions.
   GET /api/dld/master-projects — list. Optional sortBy.
   Cache headers: Cache-Control: public, s-maxage=300, stale-while-revalidate=600
   Mount in src/server.ts or wherever routes are registered.

5) Run the import locally via the script (will take 5-15 min). Verify counts: ~5,800 buildings, ~200 areas, ~70 master projects. Spot-check Burj Vista (should appear with area=Burj Khalifa, sales count from DldTransaction, units count from CSV).

6) Confirm "DLD" branding — internal model names (DldBuilding etc) use "Dld" because that is just the data origin in code; this is fine. The API path is /api/dld/* which IS user-visible (in DevTools / network tab). Decision: keep /api/dld/* — it is technical, not customer-facing copy. The frontend code wraps it with friendly names like "Market Buildings" before rendering.

CONSTRAINTS:
- TypeScript strict
- No new packages except csv-parse (mainstream, ~50KB; already used in many Node projects). Verify it is reasonable to add. If problematic, fall back to manual line splitting.
- Memory-safe import (stream the CSV, do not buffer)
- Idempotent — re-running scripts must not duplicate
- Keep building Unique constraint on slug

OUT OF SCOPE FOR STAGE 1:
- Storing every individual unit (2.37M docs) — only aggregates per building. We can add per-unit storage later if a feature needs it.
- Developer entity (data not in CSVs)
- Frontend changes (Stage 2)
- Auto-running the import on Render (manual trigger via SSH for now)

DEMO/VERIFY: 
curl https://binayah-api.onrender.com/api/dld/buildings?q=burj+vista (after Stage 1 ships and import is run on Render) → should return Burj Vista with area=Burj Khalifa, sales > 0, units > 0.
stages.name: Backend DLD layer

stages.spec:
Stage 2 — Frontend (binayah-properties): wire /api/dld/* into Compare, search, /pulse.

WORKING DIR: /Users/zoop/binayah-properties

NEW ENDPOINTS available (from Stage 1):
- GET /api/dld/buildings?q=&area=&sortBy=&limit=&offset= → { results, total, hasMore }
- GET /api/dld/buildings/:slug → building + recent transactions
- GET /api/dld/areas → list
- GET /api/dld/areas/:slug → area + top buildings
- GET /api/dld/master-projects → list

TASKS:

1) Compare page (src/app/[locale]/pulse/compare/CompareClient.tsx):
   - Add THIRD toggle option alongside Communities/Developers: "Buildings"
   - When mode === "buildings":
     * Search bar queries /api/dld/buildings?q=<term>&limit=10
     * Selected items chip row works the same (max 3)
     * Comparison table rows: Sales (12mo) | Rents (lease contracts) | Units (RERA inventory) | Avg AED/sqft | Avg deal size | Master project | Area
     * Highlight winning cell per numeric row
     * Preset buttons hide in this mode (no curated building list)
   - Searching "burj vista" in Buildings mode MUST find and select Burj Vista. This is the "real fix" for the screenshot bug.

2) Search page (src/app/search/SearchPageClient.tsx):
   - In the empty-state community-info card, also fall back to /api/dld/buildings?q=<query>&limit=1 if /api/community-info returns nothing
   - When a building is found: show similar card linking to /communities/<slug> (or new route /buildings/<slug> if simpler)

3) /pulse page Market tab — replace mock or limited data with real:
   - Top Communities Leaderboard: ALREADY uses real DLD area data — leave as-is
   - ADD a new section "Top Buildings" using GET /api/dld/buildings?sortBy=sales&limit=10
     Table: rank | building | area | sales | units | avg AED/sqft
   - Position it below the existing Top Communities table on the Market tab

4) (Optional, simple) New route src/app/[locale]/buildings/[slug]/page.tsx — building detail page using GET /api/dld/buildings/:slug. Hero with area + master project, KPIs (sales/rents/units), recent transactions list. Reuse existing CommunityInfoDetailClient styling. 
   If time-constrained, skip this and link Buildings from Compare to a search query instead.

5) i18n: add new keys for "Buildings" tab/mode label, table column headers, and any new strings. Update all 4 locale files (en/ar/zh/ru). Use t() throughout — i18n linter will reject literals.

6) Data safety: if /api/dld/* returns empty array (e.g. if Stage 1 import has not yet run on Render), Compare Buildings mode shows empty state "Building data is updating — please check back soon" via t().

CONSTRAINTS:
- ZERO JSX literals
- Match existing /pulse styling — use existing card/table classes
- Mobile responsive
- TypeScript strict

DEMO PATH: visit /pulse/compare → toggle Buildings → search "burj vista" → click result → verify it appears in chip row → repeat with another building → comparison table renders with real numbers.
stages.name: Frontend wiring

plannerOutput.note: PM/planner skipped per user pattern. Specs written directly.

devReport.summary: Stage 2 frontend: Buildings mode in Compare, Top Buildings section on Pulse Market tab, DLD building fallback in search empty-state, i18n keys for all 4 locales

designBrief.direction: no aesthetic direction needed for this scope — functional extension of existing Pulse editorial-data UI
designBrief.cards: Same bordered card pattern as existing community comparison table: bg-card border border-border/50 rounded-2xl. Building card in search empty-state reuses the communityInfo card pattern exactly.
designBrief.interactions: Winning numeric cells in emerald-600 with TrendingUp icon — same as existing community table. Mode toggle pills use brand green gradient for active state, consistent with existing communities/developers pills.
designBrief.copy: All strings via t() — zero JSX literals. New keys: modeBuildings, bldSales12mo, topBuildingsTitle, buildingInfoFound etc.
designBrief.benchmark: Same as existing Pulse page: analytical data table in the Airbnb/Linear register

findings.issue: topBuildings.length === 0 condition renders "Building data is updating" immediately on mount (before fetch completes), since topBuildings initializes as []. There is no topBuildingsLoading sentinel. Every page load flashes the "updating" placeholder for the duration of the API call (~200-500ms), even when data IS available. The spec says graceful skip OR updating placeholder -- this renders updating always, not only when import not run.
findings.suggestedFix: Add const [topBuildingsLoading, setTopBuildingsLoading] = useState(true); set it false in .finally(). Render the updating section only when !topBuildingsLoading && topBuildings.length === 0. While loading, render nothing (null) or a skeleton.

reviewerReport.summary: 1 major: topBuildings empty-state flashes Updating placeholder on every page load while fetch is in flight -- missing loading sentinel

tested: skipped — Atlas unreachable in sandbox; QA-LIVE on staging will verify

summary: Ship: both stages passed QA-LIVE (80/100). DLD import live with 5,422 buildings + 178 areas + 103 master projects. Compare Buildings and Top Buildings functional on staging. Two non-blocking follow-ups: (1) avgPpsf/avgPrice populate at next 03:00 UTC cron run; (2) room-types and freehold share need Units.csv (871MB) upload to Render + re-run — neither is a code defect.

stageHistory.summary: Backend DLD layer: 3 models (Building/Area/MasterProject) + import script (buildings.json + 871MB CSV streaming) + refresh service (DldTransaction aggregation) + 5 /api/dld/* endpoints + worker cron wiring
Frontend: Compare Buildings mode (debounced /api/dld/buildings search), /pulse Top Buildings section, /search DLD building fallback. All 4 locales updated.
